stem_template: {name} starts with {S} sweets. Every day, {name} gets {D} more sweets. Can {name} ever have exactly {target} sweets?

visual_manifest.art_brief: A number line starting at {S}, with arrows jumping by {D} each time: {S}, then {S}+{D}, then {S}+2×{D}... A circled number {target} with a question mark over it.
visual_manifest.alt_text: Number line jumping by {D} from {S}. Can we land on {target}?

misconceptions.feedback_child: List the numbers: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9... Always odd! 8 is even.
Let's check! 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11... Do you ever see 8?

socratic_feedback.on_correct: No! Starting odd and adding 2 keeps it odd forever. 8 is even, so it's impossible!
socratic_feedback.generic_incorrect: Write the sequence: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9... What do you notice about these numbers?
socratic_feedback.encouragement_after_retry: Odd + even = odd. So starting at 1 and adding 2 always stays odd!

author: AP
